## [Ralph] [PLAN] Round 1 | Step: planning
**Time**: 2026-03-24 05:41:44
**Summary**: 

[PLAN]

## Task Understanding

Two bugs in PyGAD need fixing:

1. **Requirement 1** — `adaptive_mutation_population_fitness` in `mutation.py` (lines 412–445) calls the fitness function one solution at a time even when `fitness_batch_size > 1`. The batch fitness function returns an array instead of a scalar, causing `ValueError: setting an array element with a sequence`.

2. **Requirement 2** — `GA.__init__` in `pygad.py` (lines 294–308) rejects list/tuple/ndarray for `random_mutation_min_val`/`random_mutation_max_val`. Additionally, `mutation_randomly` in `mutation.py` (lines 250–302) doesn't index into per-gene bounds.

## Proposed Approach

### Requirement 1: Batch fitness in adaptive mutation
- In `adaptive_mutation_population_fitness` (mutation.py:441–442), replace the single-solution for-loop with batch-aware logic mirroring `cal_pop_fitness` (pygad.py:1626–1653):
  - When `self.fitness_batch_size` is `None` or `1`: keep existing per-solution call
  - When `self.fitness_batch_size > 1`: collect offspring indices, split into batches of `self.fitness_batch_size`, call `self.fitness_func(self, batch_solutions, batch_indices)` per batch, scatter results back into `fitness` array

### Requirement 2: Per-gene mutation bounds
- **pygad.py (lines 294–308)**: Extend validation to accept `list`, `tuple`, `numpy.ndarray` for both params. Validate length matches `num_genes` and all elements are numeric. Reject mixed types (one scalar, one sequence). Store as-is.
- **mutation.py (lines 262–266, 294–297)**: In `mutation_randomly`, before `numpy.random.uniform`, check if bounds are sequence types and index by `gene_idx`. Pass per-gene bounds to `solve_duplicate_genes_randomly` as well.

## Expected Deliverables
- Modified `code/pygad/pygad.py` — constructor validation for mutation bounds
- Modified `code/pygad/utils/mutation.py` — batch fitness + per-gene mutation bounds

## Test Plan
- **Test command**: `docker exec -w /app/code -e PYTHONPATH=src:. <container>_rll python -m pytest tests --tb=short -q`
- **Expected**: All 10 currently failing tests pass, 114 previously passing tests remain passing (124 total pass, 0 fail)
- **New tests**: 0 — cannot modify test files per ENVIRONMENT.md rules

## Quality Gate
- No lint/format commands specified in the project; will verify via test pass/fail only

## Current State
- 10 tests failing, 114 passing (verified via test run)

## [Ralph] [FIX] Round 2 | Step: planning
**Time**: 2026-03-24 05:45:37
**Summary**: 

[FIX]

Lisa is right — the plan round had no code changes. Here are the actual implementations for both requirements.

## Explanation

Lisa correctly identified that the plan submission contained no file changes. The code still had all three bugs: single-solution fitness calls in batch mode, scalar-only validation for mutation bounds, and no per-gene indexing in `mutation_randomly`. All three are now fixed.

## Changes Made

### Requirement 1: Batch fitness in `adaptive_mutation_population_fitness` (mutation.py:441)
- Replaced the single-solution for-loop with batch-aware logic that mirrors `cal_pop_fitness` (pygad.py:1626-1653)
- When `self.fitness_batch_size` is `None` or `1`: keeps existing per-solution call (no change)
- When `self.fitness_batch_size > 1`: collects offspring indices, splits into batches of `self.fitness_batch_size`, calls `self.fitness_func(self, batch_solutions, batch_indices)` per batch, scatters results back

### Requirement 2a: Constructor validation (pygad.py:294-308)
- Extended validation to accept `list`, `tuple`, `numpy.ndarray` for both `random_mutation_min_val` and `random_mutation_max_val`
- When sequence provided: validates length matches `num_genes` and all elements are numeric
- Rejects mixed types (one scalar, one sequence) with clear TypeError
- Preserves original type (no conversion)

### Requirement 2b: Per-gene mutation bounds (mutation.py:262-300)
- In `mutation_randomly`, before `numpy.random.uniform`, checks if bounds are sequence types and indexes by `gene_idx`
- Passes per-gene `min_val`/`max_val` to `solve_duplicate_genes_randomly` as well

## Test Results
- Test command: `docker exec -w /app/code -e PYTHONPATH=src:. <container>_rll python -m pytest tests --tb=short -q`
- Exit code: 0
- Result: 124/124 passed (previously 114 passed, 10 failed)
- Regression: 114/114 previously passing tests still pass
- New tests: 0 (test files cannot be modified per ENVIRONMENT.md)
- All 9 `test_adaptive_mutation_fitness_batch_size_*` tests now pass
- `test_random_mutation_manual_call4` now passes
- `test_adaptive_mutation_fitness_batch_size_1` still passes (regression guard)

requirements.description:
The method currently evaluates fitness one solution at a time by calling
      `self.fitness_func(self, temp_population[idx], None)` in a plain for-loop
      (line 442).  When `self.fitness_batch_size` is greater than 1 the GA is
      configured with a *batch* fitness function that expects a 2-D array of
      solutions and must return a 1-D array of scalar fitness values.  Calling it
      with a single solution row returns a numpy array instead of a scalar, and the
      assignment `fitness[idx] = <array>` raises:
        "ValueError: setting an array element with a sequence."
      This is the root cause of all 9 failing `test_adaptive_mutation_fitness_batch_size_*`
      tests (batch sizes 2–10).
requirements.contract:
The method must honour `self.fitness_batch_size` when re-evaluating offspring
      fitness:

      • When `self.fitness_batch_size` is `None` or `1` (single-solution mode):
        keep the existing per-solution call:
          fitness[idx] = self.fitness_func(self, temp_population[idx], None)

      • When `self.fitness_batch_size` is an integer greater than 1 (batch mode):
        collect the indices of solutions that need re-evaluation
        (i.e., `range(len(parents_to_keep), fitness.shape[0])`), split them into
        consecutive batches of size `self.fitness_batch_size`, and for each batch
        call the fitness function once:
          batch_fitness = self.fitness_func(self, batch_solutions, batch_indices)
        where `batch_solutions` is a 2-D ndarray of the batch rows from
        `temp_population` and `batch_indices` is the corresponding index array.
        The returned `batch_fitness` (list / tuple / ndarray) must then be
        scattered back into the `fitness` array element-by-element.

      The return signature of `adaptive_mutation_population_fitness` does not
      change: it still returns `(average_fitness, fitness[len(parents_to_keep):])`.
      The batch splitting logic should mirror the pattern already used in
      `pygad.py`'s `cal_pop_fitness` (lines 1626–1653).
requirements.acceptance_tests:
tests/test_adaptive_mutation.py::test_adaptive_mutation_fitness_batch_size_2
      tests/test_adaptive_mutation.py::test_adaptive_mutation_fitness_batch_size_3
      tests/test_adaptive_mutation.py::test_adaptive_mutation_fitness_batch_size_4
      tests/test_adaptive_mutation.py::test_adaptive_mutation_fitness_batch_size_5
      tests/test_adaptive_mutation.py::test_adaptive_mutation_fitness_batch_size_6
      tests/test_adaptive_mutation.py::test_adaptive_mutation_fitness_batch_size_7
      tests/test_adaptive_mutation.py::test_adaptive_mutation_fitness_batch_size_8
      tests/test_adaptive_mutation.py::test_adaptive_mutation_fitness_batch_size_9
      tests/test_adaptive_mutation.py::test_adaptive_mutation_fitness_batch_size_10

      All nine tests must pass without modifying any test file.
      The previously passing test `test_adaptive_mutation_fitness_batch_size_1`
      must continue to pass (regression guard).

requirements.location:
1. code/pygad/pygad.py — `GA.__init__`, parameter validation block for
         `random_mutation_min_val` / `random_mutation_max_val` (lines 294–308).
      2. code/pygad/utils/mutation.py — `mutation_randomly` method
         (around lines 250–302), specifically wherever
         `numpy.random.uniform(low=self.random_mutation_min_val,
                               high=self.random_mutation_max_val, ...)` is called
         within a per-gene loop that has a `gene_idx` variable available.
requirements.description:
`GA.__init__` currently rejects any non-numeric value for
      `random_mutation_min_val` with:
        "TypeError: The expected type of the 'random_mutation_min_val' parameter
         is numeric but <class 'list'> found."
      The test `test_random_mutation_manual_call4` passes a list of 10 values
      (`[888]*10` / `[999]*10`) to specify per-gene mutation bounds, which is a
      legitimate use-case the library should support.  Two problems exist:
        (a) The constructor validation unconditionally rejects list/tuple/ndarray
            input for these two parameters.
        (b) The `mutation_randomly` method calls
            `numpy.random.uniform(low=self.random_mutation_min_val, …)[0]`
            without indexing into the list by gene, so even if validation were
            fixed the wrong bound would be used.
requirements.contract:
(a) Constructor validation (`pygad.py`):
          Accept list, tuple, and numpy.ndarray (in addition to the already-accepted
          numeric scalars) for both `random_mutation_min_val` and
          `random_mutation_max_val`.  When a sequence is provided, validate that:
            • its length equals `num_genes` (when `num_genes` is known at init time), or
              equals the number of genes inferred from `initial_population`; and
            • each element is a numeric type in `GA.supported_int_float_types`.
          If the two parameters are of mixed types (one scalar, one list), raise a
          `TypeError` with a clear message.  Store the validated values as
          `self.random_mutation_min_val` and `self.random_mutation_max_val` without
          further conversion (preserve list/array type for downstream use).

      (b) `mutation_randomly` method (`mutation.py`):
          Inside the per-gene loop (`for gene_idx in mutation_indices:`), before
          calling `numpy.random.uniform`, determine the effective lower and upper
          bounds for the current gene:
            • If `self.random_mutation_min_val` is a list/tuple/ndarray:
                min_val = self.random_mutation_min_val[gene_idx]
                max_val = self.random_mutation_max_val[gene_idx]
            • Otherwise (scalar):
                min_val = self.random_mutation_min_val
                max_val = self.random_mutation_max_val
          Use these per-gene `min_val` / `max_val` in the `numpy.random.uniform`
          call and, if `allow_duplicate_genes == False`, also pass them to
          `solve_duplicate_genes_randomly`.

      Behavioral contract for the test scenario:
          When `random_mutation_min_val=[888]*10`, `random_mutation_max_val=[999]*10`,
          and `mutation_by_replacement=True`, every mutated gene value must fall in
          the half-open interval [888, 999).
requirements.acceptance_tests:
tests/test_crossover_mutation.py::test_random_mutation_manual_call4

      The test must pass end-to-end: `pygad.GA(…)` construction must succeed,
      `ga_instance.run()` must complete, `random_mutation()` must return values
      exclusively in `range(888, 999)`.
      Previously passing `test_crossover_mutation.py` tests must not regress.
